name: gortex
description: Code intelligence engine — graph-aware MCP tools for symbol search, call-chains, blast-radius, and structural edits.

gortex.binaryPath.description: Path to the gortex executable. Leave as 'gortex' to use PATH lookup.

gortex.autoTrackWorkspace.description: Automatically offer to track newly opened workspaces with the Gortex daemon.

gortex.statusBar.enabled.description: Show the Gortex daemon health item in the status bar.

gortex.statusBar.refreshIntervalSec.description: How often the status bar polls 'gortex daemon status', in seconds. Daemon control commands trigger an immediate refresh regardless.

gortex.references.enabled.description: Use Gortex for 'Find All References' (⇧F12). Off by default — your language server may already provide this.

gortex.implementations.enabled.description: Use Gortex for 'Go to Implementations' (⌘F12). Off by default — your language server may already provide this.

gortex.hover.enabled.description: Show 'X callers · Y dependents · Z usages' when hovering a symbol. Off by default to avoid clashing with language-server hovers.

gortex.codeLens.enabled.description: Render 'X callers · Y dependents' inline above every function declaration. Off by default — polarizing visual noise.

gortex.inlayHints.enabled.description: Show '  Nc · Md' inline after function declarations (caller/dependent counts). Takes zero vertical space.

gortex.occurrences.enabled.description: When the cursor is on a symbol, faintly underline every occurrence across the workspace (cross-file). Built-in highlight only spans the open file.

gortex.gutterIcons.enabled.description: Show 🔥 (hotspot) and 💀 (dead code) icons in the editor gutter, driven by `analyze hotspots` / `analyze dead_code`.

gortex.fileDecorations.enabled.description: Color-tint files in the Explorer + tab bar based on hotspot density and dead-symbol count.

gortex.cursorStatusBar.enabled.description: Show a second status-bar item with the symbol under the cursor and its caller / dependent / usage counts.

gortex.symbolInsight.enabled.description: Add a 'Symbol Insight' tree view to the Gortex side panel — live callers / usages / dependents / implementations for whatever the cursor is on.

gortex.analyzeDiagnostics.enabled.description: Surface dead code, hotspots, and dependency cycles as info-level diagnostics in the Problems panel. Off by default — large workspaces can be noisy.

gortex.analyze.refreshIntervalMinutes.description: How often to re-run `analyze` for hotspots / dead code / cycles. These don't change moment-to-moment, so the default is conservative.

commands.title: Find Callers of Symbol Under Cursor
Find Usages of Symbol Under Cursor
Show Blast Radius of Symbol Under Cursor

viewsWelcome.contents:
The Gortex daemon isn't running, or no repositories are tracked yet.

[Start Daemon](command:gortex.daemon.start)
[Track this workspace](command:gortex.workspace.track)

Learn more in the [Getting Started walkthrough](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%22gortexhq.gortex%23gortex.gettingStarted%22).

walkthroughs.title: Get started with Gortex
walkthroughs.description: Wire up the graph-aware code intelligence engine in three steps.

steps.title: Install the Gortex CLI
steps.description:
The extension drives the local `gortex` binary.

```sh
brew install zzet/tap/gortex
```

Verify with `gortex version`.

steps.title: Start the daemon
steps.description:
The daemon holds the knowledge graph in memory and serves every MCP client at once.
[Start Daemon](command:gortex.daemon.start)

steps.title: Track this workspace
steps.description:
Tell the daemon about the repo currently open in VS Code.
[Track Current Workspace](command:gortex.workspace.track)

steps.title: Try the graph
steps.description:
Use the command palette to search symbols, find callers, or compute blast radius — or open Copilot Chat in agent mode and ask away.
[Find Symbol…](command:gortex.symbol.find)
